section.heading: Widespread disruptions

subsection.heading: General Requirements.—
subsection.chapeau: In the event of a widespread disruption, a covered air carrier shall immediately publish, via a prominent link on the air carrier’s public internet website, a clear statement indicating whether, with respect to a passenger of the air carrier whose travel is interrupted as a result of the widespread disruption, the air carrier will—

paragraph.content: arrange for air transportation on another air carrier or foreign air carrier to the passenger’s destination; and
provide for sleeping facilities inside the airport terminal.

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the following definitions apply:

paragraph.heading: Widespread disruption.—
paragraph.content: The term “widespread disruption” means, with respect to a covered air carrier, the interruption of all or the overwhelming majority of the air carrier’s systemwide flight operations, including flight delays and cancellations, as the result of the failure of 1 or more computer systems or computer networks of the air carrier.

paragraph.heading: Covered air carrier.—
paragraph.content: The term “covered air carrier” means an air carrier that provides scheduled passenger air transportation by operating an aircraft that as originally designed has a passenger capacity of 30 or more seats.

subsection.heading: Savings Provision.—
subsection.content: Nothing in this section may be construed to modify, abridge, or repeal any obligation of an air carrier under section 42301.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–254, div. B, title IV, § 428(a), Oct. 5, 2018, 132 Stat. 3341.)
